Petition To Approve Compromise Of Disputed Claim Or Pending Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor Or Person With A Disability
Off Calendar Mar-06-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of an Adult with a Disability.
Dept. 514. Judge: Cynthia Ming-Mei Lee. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty. Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

Robert A. Piering, Esq. appeared with petitioner Katrice Collins, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for Daron Noel Ervin, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
Petitioner was placed under oath and testified.
Order appointing Ms. Collins Guardian ad Litem for Daron Noel Ervin was filed on August 10, 2017.
DOES 1 through 50, inclusive, DISMISSED with prejudice at today's hearing.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and the compromise is approved as follows:

Settlement Amount - $2,500,000.00.
Attorney Fees - $1,000,000.00.
Medical Bills - $124,506.00.
Costs - $127,108.00.

The net balance of $1,248,386.00 shall be dispersed as follows: (A) $991,000.00 shall be made payable to Pacific Life & Annuity Services, Inc. for the purchase of a Structured Settlement Annuity that shall provide future benefits to Daron Ervin as set forth in Addenum A to the Order Approving the Compromise; and (B) $257,386.00 to be deposited into the Daron Ervin Special Needs Trust to be established in the Superior Court of Alameda County.

Order Approving the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of an Adult with a Disability was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Matter is CONTINUED to June 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 514 for Progress Report on the Status of the purchase of the Structured Settlement Annuity and deposit of money into the Special Needs Trust.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Medi-Cal Lien
Off Calendar Dec-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Donald De Camara 760-730-7404 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Robert Piering 916-446-1944 of Piering Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Justin Ward, Esq. 916-443-2474 of The Ward Firm for Plaintiff.
Charles Antonen, Deputy Attorney General 415-703-5500 for Claimant CA Dept. of Health Care.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court addresses the issues raised by the contesting parties. Defendant argues comparative fault and cites case. Plaintiff argues reimbursement. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF DARON ERVIN, ROCHEL HARRIS-ERVIN's Motion For Determination Of Medi-Cal Lien.

The amount of the Medi-Cal lien including the deductions for attorney's fees and costs is $121,503.99. This amount is calculated as follows: a) the total cost of past Medi-Cal services of $682,606.67 divided by b) the total value of $10,000,000 for plaintiff Daron Ervin's claim for damages for the incident without reduction for comparative fault yielding 3) a multiplier of .06826067 multiplied by 4) the gross settlement amount of $2,500,000 yielding 5) a gross lien amount of $170,651.67 multiplied by .75 6) yielding a net lien amount taking into account a 25% reduction for fees of $127,988.75 less 7) $6,484.76 for the lien's share of costs (.06826067 x $95,000). The total value of Mr. Ervin's claim is calculated as follows: 1) the midpoint of $16,000,000 in the range opined by Glenn Guenard less 2) $6,000,000 for reasonably expected future medical expenses which it is reasonably probable that will be paid by Medi-Cal. The $6,000,000 for reasonably expected future medical expenses which it is reasonably probable that will be paid by Medi-Cal is calculated as follows: 1) $6,654,305.63 in reasonably expected lifetime care expenses without increase for inflation and without decrease for present value opined by Carol Hyland less 2) $654,305.63 in reasonably expected non-medical lifetime care expenses that are included in Ms. Hyland's calculation but not reasonably probable that will be paid by Medi-Cal such as permanent home alterations, smart phone, and home maintenance. The foregoing calculations are made per Arkansas Department of Health and Human Services v. Ahlborn (2006) 547 US 268, Aguilera v. Loma Linda University Medical Center (2015) 235 Cal. App. 4th 821, Welfare & Institutions Code 14124.72 and 14124.76. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

PLAINTIFFS DARON ERVIN, ROCHEL HARRIS-ERVIN Motion To Compel Discovery Responses

RULING: The parties stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem. The matter was discussed and argued. Defendants' request for a brief stay was denied. At the conclusion of the hearing, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiffs' motion to compel discovery responses to the following written discovery is granted: Plaintiffs' request for production of documents, set two; supplemental request for production, set one; supplemental interrogatory, set one. By failing to respond to the above discovery requests, Defendant Brown has waived all objections. That Plaintiffs may have served similar discovery on Defendant Evergreen Trails, Inc., or that Plaintiffs can obtain the information sought in written discovery by way of deposition testimony, is not good cause for Defendant Brown's failure to respond to the discovery requests. Defendant Brown shall provide verified responses, without objection, to Plaintiffs' request for production of documents, set two; supplemental request for productions, set one; and supplemental interrogatory, set one, within 15 days of service of the Order with Notice of Entry. Plaintiffs' motion to have truth of matters in requests for admissions sets one and three to John Wesley Brown deemed admitted is also granted. Defendant Brown has failed to respond to Plaintiffs' requests for admissions or timely object to the same. The facts referenced in defendant's Requests for Admissions to John Wesley Brown, sets one and three, are deemed admitted. The Court has reviewed the Berg declaration submitted in support of Defendant Brown's Opposition, but the issue of capacity is not the subject of this motion and not properly before the Court. Plaintiffs' motions to compel and deem admitted are granted without prejudice to Defendant Brown's ability to bring a motion for protective order, motion to stay responding to the above discovery, or any other similar motion, pending resolution of the issue of inability or incapacity to respond. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order. Judge Pro Tem: Shoba Dandillaya; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported
